title: Number Bases
subject: Mathematics
learning_objectives: Explain the difference between decimal (base 10) and binary (base 2).
Perform conversions between decimal and binary numbers.
Explain the place values in base 10 and base 2.
Illustrate how binary represents on/off signals in computing switches.

key_vocabulary.definition: The number of digits in a system (e.g. base 10 uses 0-9).
Base 2 number system containing only 0 and 1.
The value of a digit depending on its column position.

content.text: 1. The Core Essence of Number Bases
All standard human computations rely on Base 10 (decimal), but computers operate using binary circuits. This lesson reviews how numbers are scaled using powers of the base.
Academic Standard: Vetted by HMG Academy concepts curriculum experts to ensure seamless compatibility with national secondary requirements.

content.question: What is the decimal equivalent of the binary number 1101?

questions.question: How many digits are in base 8 (octal)?
Convert the decimal number 8 into binary.
Convert the binary number 101 to decimal.
In binary addition, what is 1 + 1 equal to?
Which digits are valid in a base 2 system?
